steps.title: Spot the identifying name
steps.content: The proper name Doleschallia bisaltide directly follows the noun "species" to identify which species is meant.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
"Species" is the noun; the italic name that follows identifies which species — a restrictive appositive. No punctuation separates a noun from its restrictive identifier.
  • Choice A — no punctuation: "one specimen of the species Doleschallia bisaltide" — the name directly identifies the noun. ✓
  • Choice B — semicolon: "one specimen of the species; Doleschallia bisaltide" — a semicolon joins two independent clauses, but "Doleschallia bisaltide" is not a clause. ✗
  • Choice C — colon: "one specimen of the species: Doleschallia bisaltide" — a colon here implies a formal list or explanation, which is unnecessary and non-standard for a simple appositive. ✗
  • Choice D — comma: "one specimen of the species, Doleschallia bisaltide" — a comma treats the name as non-restrictive extra info, but it is the identifying element. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
